title: Procheiron adapter manifest file (.procheiron/manifests/*.yaml)
description: The richer per-adapter YAML manifest staged under {root}/.procheiron/manifests/. These are deliberately free-form per deployment (the claude_cowork manifest carries cowork-specific write-root keys), so additionalProperties is true. The validator (check_manifests, validate_procheiron2 lines 254-291) only reads adapter_id, status, promotion_authority, external_action_authority, and couples status to the filename: a *.proposed.yaml must NOT declare status active, and an active or authority-granting manifest must be allowlisted. Those filename/allowlist couplings are cross-file checks (validator-supplemented), not expressible here. Validate by parsing the YAML to JSON first.

trust_level.description: Should map to an AGENT_REGISTRY trust level family (read_only | draft_writer | scaffold_writer | canonical_writer | external_actor); deployments may suffix-qualify (e.g. draft_writer_profile_writer_with_narrow_roots).

status.description: A manifest in a *.proposed.yaml file must not be 'active' unless allowlisted (validator-supplemented).

promotion_authority.description: Boolean true is the only value the validator treats as granting promotion authority (and triggering the allowlist requirement). A nested mapping or descriptive string reads as false in the validator's flat line-parse (manifest_fields).

external_action_authority.description: Live manifests carry this as a descriptive string ('ask_first_...') OR a nested mapping (default/proactive_external_actions sub-keys). The validator's flat parse reads the parent key's empty value as false; only a literal boolean true grants authority and triggers the allowlist requirement.
